perturbation.original_text: 5. "Residual Information" shall mean any Confidential Information of the disclosing party which may be retained in intangible form in the minds of those individuals of the receiving party who have had proper access to such Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything else in this Agreement, the receiving party shall be free to use any Residual Information for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, the development of its own products, provided that such party shall not be entitled to disclose Residual Information to any third parties unless such disclosure is in the course of, or as part of, any disclosure of its own products or their development.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. "Residual Information" refers to any knowledge retained by individuals after exposure to Confidential Information. The receiving party may use Residual Information freely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defined 'Residual Information' as confidential information retained in intangible form, explicitly allowing its use but restricting disclosure to third parties unless part of the receiving party's product disclosure. Removing the condition regarding disclosure creates a contradiction with Section 3, which broadly prohibits disclosing Confidential Information. The modified text, by omission, suggests an unlimited right to disclose Residual Information, conflicting with Section 3's general non-disclosure obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the restriction on disclosing residual information to third parties, which was present in the original text. This omission directly contradicts Section 3, which prohibits disclosing Confidential Information. The original text allowed disclosure of residual information only in the context of disclosing the receiving party's own products, whereas the changed text suggests there is no restriction at all, leading to a direct conflict with Section 3's general non-disclosure agreement, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation type Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Each of the parties agrees that it will not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party unless otherwise specified in writing by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Neither party will alter any physical documents provided by the other.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the restriction to only 'physical documents', this change omits any restrictions on reverse engineering, decompiling, or modifying software programs contained in the Confidential Information. Section 8, regarding the return of materials, now implies software can be freely used and modified (since altering code is now allowed), but not the physical medium it's stored on, which is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. All materials (including, without limitation, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs and lists) furnished to one party by the other, and which are designated in writing to be the property of such party, shall remain the property of such party and shall be returned to it promptly at its request, together with any copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted modification of 'any software programs' contained in the Confidential Information. The changed text limits the restriction to 'physical documents'. This omission contradicts section 8, which requires the return of 'all materials', including documents, drawings, models, etc. By removing the restriction on modifying software, the changed text implies that software can be freely used and modified, as long as the physical medium is returned, creating a contradiction. Therefore, it is a YES.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Confidential Information of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Confidential Information falls within Sections 4 or 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement governs communications between the parties indefinitely. Sections 2 and 3 remain in effect.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that allowed either party to terminate the agreement with written notice, this creates a perpetual agreement. This contradicts the implied right of parties to terminate agreements, especially if business circumstances change, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This makes the contract in section 10 even more difficult to handle since it mentions about 'This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement. This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the United States of America and by the laws of the State of California as such laws are applied to agreements entered into and to be performed entirely within California between California residents. This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement of authorized representatives of the parties in writing. All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be by personal delivery, telegram, telex, telecopier, facsimile transmission or by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, and shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, five (5) days after deposit in the mail, or upon acknowledgment of receipt of electronic transmission. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth at the end of this Agreement or such other address as either party may specify in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9 allowed for termination of the agreement with written notice. The changed text removes this termination clause, making the agreement indefinite. Section 10 states 'This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement'. If section 9 makes the agreement indefinite, that severely restricts the ability for section 10 to be enacted, as it makes any attempt to change the agreement nearly impossible. This creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement. This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the United States of America and by the laws of the State of California as such laws are applied to agreements entered into and to be performed entirely within California between California residents. This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement of authorized representatives of the parties in writing. All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be by personal delivery, telegram, telex, telecopier, facsimile transmission or by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, and shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, five (5) days after deposit in the mail, or upon acknowledgment of receipt of electronic transmission. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth at the end of this Agreement or such other address as either party may specify in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware. This Agreement may be altered by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the governing law to Delaware and permitting unilateral amendment with 30 days' notice, this alteration directly contradicts the original clause stating, 'This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement of authorized representatives of the parties in writing'. The new term permitting one-sided changes conflicts the pre existing need for mutual agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware. This Agreement may be altered by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 10 to allow unilateral changes to the agreement with 30 days' notice. The original text required mutual agreement for any changes. This directly contradicts the previous requirement for mutual agreement, as explained in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, and since the changed text does indeed contradict the previous clause requiring mutual agreement for changes, the change introduces a strong contradiction.
